title: tithymalus esula subsp. riparia
intro: Tithymalus esula subsp. riparia, also known as the riparian leafy spurge, is a subspecies of the leafy spurge plant, which is a type of perennial herb in the Euphorbiaceae family. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Tithymalus esula subsp. riparia include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and supporting wound healing, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, which are typically prepared by steeping the leaves and roots in hot water or oil.
